---
title: "Research"
---

<a class="lang-switch" href="/zh/research.html">中文</a>

<section class="panel panel-light">
<h2>Introduction</h2>
<p>My research sits at the intersection of economic geography, urban and regional economics, and science of science. I study how scientific mobility, institutional affiliations, collaboration relations, and city-level research systems are associated with the spatial organization of knowledge production.</p>
<p>The common thread is empirical work that treats scientists, institutions, publications, and cities as linked but distinct units of analysis. This helps separate observed affiliations, employment histories, collaboration ties, and mobility events before applying statistical or network models.</p>
</section>

<section class="panel panel-muted">
<h2>Research Themes</h2>
<p><strong>Scientific Mobility and Career Trajectories.</strong> I examine how elite biomedical scientists move across institutions and cities over time. Current work links career histories with publication-location exposure and destination choices while keeping the observational design explicit.</p>
<p><strong>Regional Innovation and Urban Networks.</strong> I study how cities and regions differ in research capacity, institutional diversity, and positions within mobility and collaboration networks. This work connects urban research systems with observed patterns of high-skilled mobility and knowledge production.</p>
<p><strong>Science of Science and Relational Data.</strong> I build and audit datasets that connect scientists, institutions, publications, and places. The goal is to make relational data usable for research on scientific geography while keeping linkage, coverage, and geocoding uncertainty visible.</p>
</section>

<section class="panel panel-light">
<h2>Data and Methods</h2>
<ul>
<li>Longitudinal scientist-institution-city data</li>
<li>Discrete-choice models</li>
<li>Network analysis</li>
<li>Spatial data methods</li>
<li>Reproducible workflows in Python and R</li>
</ul>
</section>

<section class="panel panel-dark">
<h2>Current Research Agenda</h2>
<p><strong>Destination choice among elite biomedical scientists.</strong> This project studies whether a scientist's pre-move publication-location exposure is related to the destination city selected from an event-specific candidate set.</p>
<p><strong>Mobility and collaboration networks.</strong> This line of work extends earlier thesis and article research on intercity mobility networks by developing longitudinal scientist-institution-city data and directed weighted network indicators.</p>
<p><strong>Public workflow audit for author-institution-city linkage.</strong> The public repository uses synthetic records to demonstrate schema validation, institution normalization, geocoding checks, and documentation patterns for reproducible research workflows.</p>
</section>
